claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with the basics:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex. He's 19 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 19 years old, I need to ask you a few questions to determine eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

(For example, this could include A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs up to level 3, or similar qualifications)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's doing his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Since Alex is 19 and in approved education (A-levels), this is relevant to his eligibility.

**One more important question: Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for providing that information. Let me assess Alex's eligibility based on what you've told me.

You've confirmed that:
- You have one child, Alex, who is 19 years old
- Alex lives with you at home
- Alex is currently doing his A-levels (approved education)
- You live in the UK (Manchester)

Based on the Child Benefit rules, you would normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. However, you can still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 19, still under 20, and is in approved education (A-levels), you should be eligible for Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Since Alex is 19 years old, under 20, and is currently in approved education (his A-levels), you qualify for Child Benefit. The fact that he lives with you in the UK and you're responsible for him means you meet all the necessary criteria.

**Important things to note:**
- Your eligibility will continue as long as Alex remains in approved education or training and is under 20 years old
- If Alex finishes his A-levels or leaves education, or when he turns 20, your Child Benefit will stop
- If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit. If your circumstances change (like Alex leaving education), make sure to inform HMRC.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 19 years old and currently doing his A-levels, which counts as approved education. Under Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for a child under 16, but eligibility extends to children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is under 20 (he's 19) and is in approved education (A-levels), the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK (Manchester), confirming they are responsible for him and meet the residency requirement.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 19-year-old son Alex. Although Alex is over 16, he remains eligible because he is under 20 and is currently in approved education (A-levels). The claimant lives with Alex in the UK and is responsible for him, meeting all the necessary criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.
